title: Exodus  of Sri Lanka’s Talented IT Professionals May Gather Momentum if the Island Nation’s Economic Crisis Shows No Sign Of Being Resolved
body_lines: There have been days when cybersecurity professional Asela Waidyalankara and his colleagues have sat in hotel lobbies to complete projects during power outages. Other days, they have run around Colombo looking for fuel for generators so they could work from home.

“We have a buddy system at the company to inform each other about fuel availability,” laughs Waidyalankara, adding that his company encourages staff to carpool if they have to attend meetings in Colombo and work from home when possible.

Before the pandemic, Sri Lanka’s IT industry employed more than 120,000 people and was the fifth-largest export earner for the island nation of 22 million. It was on track to become the top exporter within the next five years and double its employees. But with the government of President Gotabaya Rajapaksa having defaulted on its foreign debt earlier this year and critical shortages crippling the economy, those plans are now in jeopardy as it becomes harder to maintain normal business operations.

Daily, hours-long power cuts are now normal. Fuel queues stretch for kilometres, sometimes so far that one fuel queue meets another. The country is running on a cash flow basis. “We are using whatever dollars that flow in to purchase the essentials we can,” Nandalal Weerasinghe, governor of the Central Bank of Sri Lanka (CBSL), said last month.

On May 19, the CBSL said it expects the economy to “record a setback” even as the cost of living continues to shoot up – May inflation was 39.1 percent, and fuel prices have more than doubled since the start of the year.

Steady power and smooth internet, essential for the IT industry to function effectively, are constantly affected. “We have weekly and monthly deliverables and we struggle to meet them sometimes,” Waidyalankara told Al Jazeera. “Our service levels are also affected, for example, we have to reply to a cybersecurity incident within two hours, but with power cuts and slow internet it is a challenge to meet these expectations,” he said.

Sri Lanka has several regional competitors in the IT sector, including India, Bangladesh and Vietnam. “We have spent years cultivating relationships with clients, so they are understanding, but there is always a fear that our business will be taken away and given to competitors if we cannot maintain our delivery and quality,” says Waidyalankara.
The ongoing economic crisis and the lack of a cohesive plan by the incumbent government to resolve it are starting to affect the confidence of foreign investors.

Sanjiva Weerawarana, founder and chief executive of WSO2, a Sri Lankan software company which raised $90m from Goldman Sachs in November, told Al Jazeera that his company has had to look for creative ways to hedge the country’s risk to appease investors. While WSO2 has offices in several countries, including the United States, the United Kingdom, Dubai and India, the company will “likely expand” its presence outside Sri Lanka to boost investor confidence and ensure business continuity, Weerawarana said.

Some companies have even set up temporary offices in neighbouring countries like India and Dubai and relocated some of their staff to these offices to maintain business continuity. “We are not going to relocate the company, but we temporarily relocated a few staff members to a Dubai office in April just to ensure business runs smoothly,” said Weerawarana.

The past few years in Sri Lanka have been troublesome. An unconstitutional 52-day government in October 2018 shook investor confidence and was followed a few months later by deadly bomb blasts on Easter Sunday in April 2019. And while migration, especially among highly skilled workers like software engineers, doctors and nurses is not uncommon, the economic conditions of the past year, on top of the turmoil before that, have increased the pace at which Sri Lankans seek greener pastures.

The ongoing economic crisis has made the cost of living exorbitant, especially as the value of the US dollar has appreciated by 75 percent against the Sri Lankan rupee in the past year. Owning a car or a house is a pipe dream and with their hopes crumbling around them, many young Sri Lankans are looking to migrate and get away from an uncertain future in their birth country.

A November 2021 survey report (PDF) by the Colombo-based Institute for Health Policy, an independent research centre, found that the number of Sri Lankans who want to migrate had doubled from three to five years ago and nearly 50 percent of the young and educated wanted to migrate now. Long queues at the passport office are just one indication of this.

A 35-year old software engineer who preferred not to be named moved to Australia with his family in March this year. “I didn’t really have any plans to migrate. I ran a small IT company, and I was happy with it,” he said. But a rise in ethnic and racial tensions in the country before the 2019 elections made him think about relocating and the subsequent economic mismanagement sealed his decision. “It’s really not promising in Sri Lanka. I want to prioritize my daughter’s future,” he said.

If Sri Lankan software companies can power some of the world’s largest stock markets like the London Stock Exchange and Borsa Italiana, and some of the world’s busiest airlines like Qantas, it is because of local talent. A brain drain would spell disaster for the sector.

“It’s our people who have helped us build world-class companies,” says Waidyalankara. “The real competitive edge for our industry comes from people, and unfortunately they are now beginning to move out of the country. It is a big loss.”
Weerawarana agrees. “We believe that 10 percent of our technically skilled and experienced staff have already applied for migration and another 20-40 percent are probably considering it,” he said.

The industry has also been important in other ways – it was one of the first to publicly support the wave of spontaneous protests held across Sri Lanka in March and April this year against the Rajapaksa government. Industry personnel even organized a rally to mark 50 days of protests since locals set up the ‘GotaGoGama’ site at the capital’s Galle Face Green promenade.

With its high salaries and flexible working environment, Sri Lanka’s IT industry has always had a better draw of talent than other important sectors like banking and tourism. To retain that talent now, industry players have had to devise newer ways.

Since the Sri Lankan rupee began plummeting, several IT companies which earn in foreign currencies have begun pegging the salaries of their local staff to either the US dollar, the pound sterling, the euro or the Australian dollar – making the industry an attractive employer for many locals.

This has had an unexpected effect on the smaller IT firms which cater to local clients and lack a large pipeline of foreign contracts and currencies. Unlike the larger companies, they are not able to peg the salaries of their staff to a foreign currency.

“Some of the key staff are considering migration or are moving into the larger companies who pay dollar-pegged salaries,” Deane Jayamanne, an owner of a small software company, told Al Jazeera. “On top of power cuts and internet problems, we also have to deal with this now,” he added.

The software engineer who migrated to Australia said that he did not think the IT industry in Sri Lanka had proper talent acquisition and retention policies. “When I applied for jobs to migrate [a year ago], I got two offers from Australia and the Netherlands. They took care of everything. The visa, air tickets, relocation expenses and even temporary housing. All I had to do was show up at the airport. That is how far they go to acquire talent.”

Despite ambitious plans last year by Sri Lanka’s apex body for investment promotion in the country, the Board of Investment, to push the IT industry as a top earner and employer for the nation, the reality seems to be heading another way.

The situation is aggravated because there was already an existing supply gap in the industry. The Information and Communication Technology Agency, the government body responsible for ICT policy in the country, warned in a 2019 report that “the demand-supply gap for ICT workers is widening rather than closing”.

Even with dollar-pegged salaries and flexible work environments, a quick resolution to the ongoing economic crisis is what is needed to retain talent, employers say. It is also the key to ensuring a steady pipeline of foreign investors and clients. “Once we have charted a course for the country, I believe investor trouble will go down significantly,” said Weerawarana.

Experts worry that without that solution, the country “risks undermining its future growth”. Economist Malathy Knight, in an interview with Al Jazeera, pointed to Lebanon, which saw a significant exodus of local talent in the face of its own economic and political crisis. “Sri Lanka should learn from the experiences of other countries,” she said.

“It is vital to provide hope and opportunities to the youth”, Knight said, “without such efforts, Sri Lanka will continue to spiral downward economically and socially even after the economic crisis stabilizes.”

si.title: දිවයිනේ ජාතියේ ආර්ථික අර්බුදය විසඳෙන ලකුණු නොපෙන්වන්නේ නම්, ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ දක්ෂ තොරතුරු තාක්ෂණ වෘත්තිකයන්ගේ නික්මයාම වේගවත් විය හැක.
body_lines: සයිබර් ආරක්ෂණ වෘත්තිකයෙකු වන අසේල වෛද්‍යාලංකාර සහ ඔහුගේ සගයන් විදුලිය ඇනහිටීම්වලදී ව්‍යාපෘති නිම කිරීමට හෝටල් ලොබිවල වාඩි වූ දින තිබේ. අනිත් දවස් වල ගෙදර ඉඳන් වැඩ කරන්න පුළුවන් නිසා ජෙනරේටර් වලට තෙල් හොයන්න කොළඹ වටේ දුවනවා.

“ඉන්ධන ලබා ගැනීමේ හැකියාව පිළිබඳව එකිනෙකා දැනුවත් කිරීමට අප ආයතනයේ මිත්‍රත්ව පද්ධතියක් ඇත,” වෛද්‍යාලංකාර සිනාසෙමින්, තම සමාගම කාර්ය මණ්ඩලයට කොළඹ රැස්වීම්වලට සහභාගී වීමට සහ හැකි සෑම විටම නිවසේ සිට වැඩ කිරීමට සිදුවුවහොත් කාර්පූල් කිරීමට ඔවුන්ව දිරිමත් කරන බව පැවසීය.

වසංගතයට පෙර, ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ තොරතුරු තාක්ෂණ කර්මාන්තය 120,000 කට වැඩි පිරිසක් සේවය කළ අතර මිලියන 22 ක් වූ දූපත් ජාතිය සඳහා පස්වන විශාලතම අපනයන උපයන්නා විය. එය ඉදිරි වසර පහ තුළ ඉහළම අපනයනකරු බවට පත් වීමටත් එහි සේවකයින් දෙගුණ කිරීමටත් ගමන් කරමින් සිටියේය. එහෙත් ජනාධිපති ගෝඨාභය රාජපක්ෂගේ රජය මේ වසර මුලදී සිය විදේශ ණය පැහැර හැරීම සහ බරපතල හිඟයන් ආර්ථිකය අඩපණ කිරීමත් සමඟ සාමාන්‍ය ව්‍යාපාරික මෙහෙයුම් පවත්වාගෙන යාම දුෂ්කර වන බැවින් එම සැලසුම් දැන් අනතුරට පත්ව ඇත.

දිනපතා, පැය ගණන් විදුලිය කැපීම දැන් සාමාන්‍ය තත්ත්වයකි. ඉන්ධන පෝලිම් කිලෝමීටර් ගණනක් දිගු වේ, සමහර විට එක් ඉන්ධන පෝලිමක් තවත් ඉන්ධන පෝලිමක් හමු වේ. රට දුවන්නේ මුදල් ප්‍රවාහ පදනමක් මතයි. “අපට හැකි අත්‍යවශ්‍ය ද්‍රව්‍ය මිලදී ගැනීම සඳහා ගලා එන ඕනෑම ඩොලර් අපි භාවිතා කරමු,” ශ්‍රී ලංකා මහ බැංකුවේ (CBSL) අධිපති නන්දලාල් වීරසිංහ පසුගිය මාසයේ පැවසීය.
මැයි 19දා, CBSL පැවසුවේ, ජීවන වියදම අඛණ්ඩව ඉහළ යමින් තිබියදීත් ආර්ථිකය "පසුබැසීමක්" අපේක්ෂා කරන බව - මැයි උද්ධමනය සියයට 39.1 ක් වූ අතර වසර ආරම්භයේ සිට ඉන්ධන මිල දෙගුණයකට වඩා වැඩි වී ඇත.

තොරතුරු තාක්ෂණ කර්මාන්තය ඵලදායි ලෙස ක්‍රියාත්මක වීමට අත්‍යවශ්‍ය ස්ථාවර බලය සහ සුමට අන්තර්ජාලය නිරන්තරයෙන් බලපායි. "අපට සතිපතා සහ මාසික බෙදාහැරීම් ඇති අතර සමහර විට ඒවා හමුවීමට අපි වෙහෙසෙමු," වෛද්‍යාලංකාර අල් ජසීරාට පැවසීය. "අපගේ සේවා මට්ටම් ද බලපායි, උදාහරණයක් ලෙස, අපට පැය දෙකක් ඇතුළත සයිබර් ආරක්ෂණ සිද්ධියකට පිළිතුරු දිය යුතු නමුත්, විදුලිය විසන්ධි කිරීම සහ මන්දගාමී අන්තර්ජාලය සමඟ මෙම අපේක්ෂාවන් සපුරාලීම අභියෝගයක්," ඔහු පැවසීය.

ඉන්දියාව, බංග්ලාදේශය සහ වියට්නාමය ඇතුළු තොරතුරු තාක්ෂණ ක්ෂේත්‍රයේ කලාපීය තරඟකරුවන් කිහිප දෙනෙකු ශ්‍රී ලංකාවට සිටී. "අපි ගනුදෙනුකරුවන් සමඟ සබඳතා ගොඩනඟා ගැනීමට වසර ගණනාවක් ගත කර ඇත, එබැවින් ඔවුන් තේරුම් ගෙන ඇත, නමුත් අපගේ බෙදා හැරීම සහ ගුණාත්මකභාවය පවත්වා ගැනීමට නොහැකි නම් අපගේ ව්‍යාපාරය පැහැරගෙන තරඟකරුවන්ට ලබා දෙනු ඇතැයි යන බිය සැමවිටම පවතී," වෛද්‍යාලංකාර පවසයි.
පවතින ආර්ථික අර්බුදය සහ එය විසඳීමට වත්මන් රජයට ඒකාබද්ධ සැලැස්මක් නොමැතිකම විදේශීය ආයෝජකයින්ගේ විශ්වාසයට බලපෑම් කිරීමට පටන් ගෙන තිබේ.

නොවැම්බර් මාසයේදී Goldman Sachs වෙතින් ඩොලර් මිලියන 90ක් උපයාගත් ශ්‍රී ලාංකික මෘදුකාංග සමාගමක් වන WSO2 හි නිර්මාතෘ සහ ප්‍රධාන විධායක සංජිව වීරවරන Al Jazeera වෙත පැවසුවේ ආයෝජකයින් සතුටු කිරීම සඳහා රටේ අවදානම ආරක්ෂා කිරීම සඳහා නිර්මාණාත්මක ක්‍රම සෙවීමට තම සමාගමට සිදුව ඇති බවයි. WSO2 හි එක්සත් ජනපදය, එක්සත් රාජධානිය, ඩුබායි සහ ඉන්දියාව ඇතුළු රටවල් කිහිපයක කාර්යාල ඇති අතර, සමාගම ශ්‍රී ලංකාවෙන් පිටත ආයෝජකයින්ගේ විශ්වාසය ඉහළ නැංවීමට සහ ව්‍යාපාරික අඛණ්ඩතාව සහතික කිරීම සඳහා "පුළුල්" කිරීමට ඉඩ ඇති බව වීරවරණ පැවසීය.

සමහර සමාගම් ඉන්දියාව සහ ඩුබායි වැනි අසල්වැසි රටවල තාවකාලික කාර්යාල පවා පිහිටුවා ඇති අතර ව්‍යාපාර අඛණ්ඩව පවත්වාගෙන යාම සඳහා ඔවුන්ගේ සමහර කාර්ය මණ්ඩලය මෙම කාර්යාල වෙත මාරු කර ඇත. "අපි සමාගම වෙනත් ස්ථානයකට ගෙන යාමට යන්නේ නැත, නමුත් ව්‍යාපාර කටයුතු සුමටව සිදු කිරීම සහතික කිරීම සඳහා අපි අප්‍රේල් මාසයේදී කාර්ය මණ්ඩල සාමාජිකයින් කිහිප දෙනෙකු තාවකාලිකව ඩුබායි කාර්යාලයකට මාරු කළෙමු," වීරවරණ පැවසීය.

ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ පසුගිය වසර කිහිපය කරදරකාරී විය. 2018 ඔක්තෝම්බර් මාසයේ ව්‍යවස්ථා විරෝධී දින 52ක ආන්ඩුවක් ආයෝජකයින්ගේ විශ්වාසය බිඳ දැමූ අතර මාස කිහිපයකට පසුව 2019 අප්‍රේල් මාසයේ පාස්කු ඉරිදා මාරාන්තික බෝම්බ පිපිරීම් සිදු විය. තවද සංක්‍රමණය අතරතුර, විශේෂයෙන් මෘදුකාංග ඉංජිනේරුවන්, වෛද්‍යවරුන් සහ හෙදියන් වැනි ඉහළ කුසලතා ඇති කම්කරුවන් අතර සංක්‍රමණය සාමාන්‍ය දෙයක් නොවේ. පසුගිය වසරේ ආර්ථික තත්ත්වයන්, ඊට පෙර පැවති කැළඹීම් මත, ශ්‍රී ලාංකිකයන් හරිත තණබිම් සොයන වේගය වැඩි කර ඇත.

විශේෂයෙන්ම පසුගිය වසර තුළ ශ්‍රී ලංකා රුපියලට සාපේක්ෂව එක්සත් ජනපද ඩොලරයේ අගය සියයට 75 කින් ඉහළ ගොස් ඇති බැවින් පවතින ආර්ථික අර්බුදය ජීවන වියදම අධික වී ඇත. මෝටර් රථයක් හෝ නිවසක් හිමිකර ගැනීම සිහිනයක් වන අතර, ඔවුන්ගේ බලාපොරොත්තු ඔවුන් වටා කඩා වැටීමත් සමඟ, බොහෝ ශ්‍රී ලාංකික තරුණයින් සංක්‍රමණය වීමට සහ තම උපන් රටේ අවිනිශ්චිත අනාගතයකින් මිදීමට අපේක්ෂා කරයි.

ස්වාධීන පර්යේෂණ මධ්‍යස්ථානයක් වන කොළඹ පදනම් වූ සෞඛ්‍ය ප්‍රතිපත්ති සඳහා වූ ආයතනය විසින් 2021 නොවැම්බර් මස සමීක්ෂණ වාර්තාවක් (PDF) මගින් සොයාගෙන ඇත්තේ සංක්‍රමණය වීමට අවශ්‍ය ශ්‍රී ලාංකිකයින් සංඛ්‍යාව වසර තුනේ සිට පහකට පෙර සිට දෙගුණයක් වී ඇති අතර තරුණයන්ගෙන් සියයට 50 කට ආසන්න ප්‍රමාණයක් සහ උගත් අයට දැන් සංක්‍රමණය වීමට අවශ්‍ය විය. විදේශ ගමන් බලපත්‍ර කාර්යාලයේ දිගු පෝලිම් මෙහි එක් ඇඟවීමක් පමණි.

නම සඳහන් නොකිරීමට කැමති 35 හැවිරිදි මෘදුකාංග ඉංජිනේරුවෙක් මේ වසරේ මාර්තු මාසයේදී තම පවුලේ අය සමඟ ඕස්ට්‍රේලියාවට ගියේය. “මට ඇත්තටම සංක්‍රමණය වීමට කිසිදු සැලසුමක් තිබුණේ නැත. මම කුඩා තොරතුරු තාක්ෂණ සමාගමක් පවත්වාගෙන ගිය අතර, මම එයින් සතුටු වුණා, ”ඔහු පැවසීය. නමුත් 2019 මැතිවරණයට පෙර රට තුළ ජනවාර්ගික හා වාර්ගික ආතතීන් ඉහළ යාම ඔහු නැවත පදිංචි කිරීම ගැන සිතීමට හේතු වූ අතර පසුව ඇති වූ ආර්ථික වැරදි කළමනාකරණය ඔහුගේ තීරණයට මුද්‍රා තැබීය. “එය ඇත්ත වශයෙන්ම ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ පොරොන්දු නොවේ. මට මගේ දුවගේ අනාගතයට මුල්තැන දෙන්න අවශ්‍යයි,” ඔහු පැවසීය.

ලන්ඩන් කොටස් හුවමාරුව සහ Borsa Italiana වැනි ලෝකයේ විශාලතම කොටස් වෙළඳපොලවල් සහ Qantas වැනි ලෝකයේ කාර්යබහුලම ගුවන් සමාගම් කිහිපයක් බල ගැන්වීමට ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ මෘදුකාංග සමාගම්වලට හැකි නම්, ඒ දේශීය දක්ෂතා නිසාය. බුද්ධි ගලනයක් අංශයට ව්‍යසනයක් ඇති කරයි.

“ලෝක මට්ටමේ සමාගම් ගොඩනඟන්න අපට උදව් කළේ අපේ මිනිස්සු,” වෛද්‍යාලංකාර පවසයි. "අපගේ කර්මාන්තයේ සැබෑ තරඟකාරීත්වය පැමිණෙන්නේ මිනිසුන්ගෙන් වන අතර, අවාසනාවකට ඔවුන් දැන් රටින් පිටවීමට පටන් ගෙන තිබේ. ඒක ලොකු පාඩුවක්.”

වීරවරණ එකඟ වේ. "අපගේ තාක්‍ෂණිකව නිපුණතා සහ පළපුරුදු කාර්ය මණ්ඩලයෙන් සියයට 10ක් දැනටමත් සංක්‍රමණය සඳහා ඉල්ලුම් කර ඇති බවත් තවත් සියයට 20-40ක් බොහෝ විට ඒ ගැන සලකා බලමින් සිටින බවත් අපි විශ්වාස කරනවා" ඔහු පැවසීය.

කර්මාන්තය වෙනත් ආකාරවලින් ද වැදගත් වී ඇත - එය රාජපක්ෂ රජයට එරෙහිව මේ වසරේ මාර්තු සහ අප්‍රේල් මාසවල ශ්‍රී ලංකාව පුරා පැවති ස්වයංසිද්ධ විරෝධතා රැල්ලට ප්‍රසිද්ධියේ සහාය දුන් පළමු එකකි. අගනුවර ගාලුමුවදොර සක්මන් මළුවේදී ප්‍රදේශවාසීන් 'ගෝඨාගෝගම' අඩවිය පිහිටවූ දා සිට විරෝධතා දින 50ක් සපිරීම නිමිත්තෙන් කර්මාන්ත කරුවන් රැලියක් පවා සංවිධානය කළේය.

ඉහළ වැටුප් සහ නම්‍යශීලී සේවා පරිසරයක් සමඟින්, ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ තොරතුරු තාක්ෂණ කර්මාන්තය බැංකුකරණය සහ සංචාරක ව්‍යාපාරය වැනි අනෙකුත් වැදගත් ක්ෂේත්‍රවලට වඩා හොඳ දක්ෂතා ඇති කර තිබේ. දැන් එම දක්ෂතාවය රඳවා ගැනීමට, කර්මාන්තයේ ක්‍රීඩකයින්ට නව ක්‍රම සැකසීමට සිදුවී ඇත.

ශ්‍රී ලංකා රුපියල කඩා වැටීමට පටන් ගත් දා සිට, විදේශ මුදල්වලින් උපයන තොරතුරු තාක්ෂණ සමාගම් කිහිපයක් තම දේශීය කාර්ය මණ්ඩලයේ වැටුප් එක්සත් ජනපද ඩොලර්, ස්ටර්ලින් පවුම්, යුරෝ හෝ ඕස්ට්‍රේලියානු ඩොලරයට සම්බන්ධ කිරීමට පටන් ගෙන ඇත - කර්මාන්තය බොහෝ දෙනෙකුට ආකර්ෂණීය සේවා යෝජකයෙකු බවට පත් කරයි. දේශීය ජනතාව.

මෙය දේශීය ගනුදෙනුකරුවන්ට සේවා සපයන කුඩා තොරතුරු තාක්ෂණ ආයතනවලට අනපේක්ෂිත බලපෑමක් ඇති කර ඇති අතර විදේශීය කොන්ත්‍රාත්තු සහ මුදල් විශාල ප්‍රමාණයක් නොමැත. විශාල සමාගම් මෙන් නොව, ඔවුන්ගේ කාර්ය මණ්ඩලයේ වැටුප් විදේශ මුදලකට සම්බන්ධ කිරීමට ඔවුන්ට හැකියාවක් නැත.

කුඩා මෘදුකාංග සමාගමක හිමිකරුවෙකු වන ඩීන් ජයමාන්න අල් ජසීරාට පැවසුවේ “සමහර ප්‍රධාන කාර්ය මණ්ඩලය සංක්‍රමණය ගැන සලකා බලමින් හෝ ඩොලර්වලින් වැටුප් ගෙවන විශාල සමාගම් වෙත මාරු වෙමින් සිටිති. "විදුලි විසන්ධි කිරීම් සහ අන්තර්ජාල ගැටළු මත, අපට දැන් මෙයට මුහුණ දීමට සිදුවී ඇත," ඔහු වැඩිදුරටත් පැවසීය.

ඔස්ට්‍රේලියාවට සංක්‍රමණය වූ මෘදුකාංග ඉංජිනේරුවරයා කියා සිටියේ ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ තොරතුරු තාක්ෂණ ක්ෂේත්‍රයට නිසි දක්ෂතා අත්පත් කර ගැනීමේ සහ රඳවා ගැනීමේ ප්‍රතිපත්ති තිබේ යැයි තමන් නොසිතන බවයි. “මම [වසරකට පෙර] සංක්‍රමණය වීමට රැකියා සඳහා ඉල්ලුම් කළ විට, මට ඕස්ට්‍රේලියාවෙන් සහ නෙදර්ලන්තයෙන් දීමනා දෙකක් ලැබුණි. ඔවුන් හැම දෙයක්ම බලාගත්තා. වීසා, ගුවන් ටිකට්පත්, නැවත ස්ථානගත කිරීමේ වියදම් සහ තාවකාලික නිවාස පවා. මට කරන්න තිබුණේ ගුවන් තොටුපළට පැමිණීම පමණයි. ඒ තරමටම ඔවුන් දක්ෂතා අත්කර ගැනීමට යනවා.”

රට තුළ ආයෝජන ප්‍රවර්ධනය සඳහා ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ ඉහළම ආයතනය වන ආයෝජන මණ්ඩලය විසින් තොරතුරු තාක්ෂණ කර්මාන්තය ජාතියේ ඉහළම ආදායම් ලබන්නෙකු සහ සේවා යෝජකයෙකු ලෙස තල්ලු කිරීම සඳහා පසුගිය වසරේ අභිලාෂකාමී සැලසුම් තිබියදීත්, යථාර්ථය වෙනත් මාර්ගයකට ගමන් කරන බව පෙනේ.

කර්මාන්තයේ දැනටමත් පවත්නා සැපයුම් පරතරයක් පැවතීම නිසා තත්වය වඩාත් නරක අතට හැරේ. තොරතුරු හා සන්නිවේදන තාක්ෂණ නියෝජිතායතනය, රටෙහි තොරතුරු හා සන්නිවේදන තාක්ෂණ ප්‍රතිපත්ති සඳහා වගකිව යුතු රජයේ ආයතනය, 2019 වාර්තාවක අනතුරු ඇඟවූයේ "තොරතුරු හා සන්නිවේදන තාක්ෂණ සේවකයින් සඳහා ඉල්ලුම-සැපයුම් පරතරය වැසීමට වඩා පුළුල් වෙමින් පවතින" බවයි.

ඩොලරය මත පදනම් වූ වැටුප් සහ නම්‍යශීලී සේවා පරිසරයන් සමඟ වුවද, පවතින ආර්ථික අර්බුදයට ඉක්මන් විසඳුමක් අවශ්‍ය වන්නේ දක්ෂතා රඳවා තබා ගැනීමට බව සේවා යෝජකයෝ පවසති. විදේශීය ආයෝජකයින්ගේ සහ ගනුදෙනුකරුවන්ගේ ස්ථාවර නල මාර්ගයක් සහතික කිරීම සඳහා ද එය යතුරයි. "අපි රට වෙනුවෙන් පාඨමාලාවක් සකස් කළ පසු, ආයෝජකයින්ගේ ගැටළු සැලකිය යුතු ලෙස අඩු වනු ඇතැයි මම විශ්වාස කරමි," වීරවරණ පැවසීය.

එම විසඳුම නොමැතිව රට “එහි අනාගත වර්ධනය අඩපණ කිරීමේ අවදානමක්” ඇති බව විශේෂඥයෝ කනස්සල්ලට පත්ව සිටිති. ආර්ථික විද්‍යාඥ මාලතී නයිට්, අල් ජසීරා සමඟ සම්මුඛ සාකච්ඡාවකදී, තමන්ගේම ආර්ථික හා දේශපාලන අර්බුදය හමුවේ දේශීය දක්ෂතා සැලකිය යුතු ලෙස නික්මයාම දුටු ලෙබනනය පෙන්වා දුන්නේය. “ශ්‍රී ලංකාව වෙනත් රටවල අත්දැකීම්වලින් ඉගෙන ගත යුතුයි,” ඇය පැවසුවාය.
"තරුණයන්ට බලාපොරොත්තු සහ අවස්ථාවන් ලබා දීම අත්‍යවශ්‍ය වේ", නයිට් පැවසුවේ, "එවැනි උත්සාහයකින් තොරව, ආර්ථික අර්බුදය ස්ථාවර වීමෙන් පසුව පවා ශ්‍රී ලංකාව ආර්ථික හා සමාජීය වශයෙන් පහතට දඟලනු ඇත."

ta.title: தீவு தேசத்தின் பொருளாதார நெருக்கடி தீர்க்கப்படுவதற்கான எந்த அறிகுறியையும் காட்டவில்லை என்றால், இலங்கையின் திறமையான தகவல் தொழில்நுட்ப வல்லுநர்களின் வெளியேற்றம் வேகம் கூடும்.
body_lines: இணைய பாதுகாப்பு நிபுணர் அசேல வைத்தியலங்காரவும் அவரது சகாக்களும் மின் தடையின் போது திட்டங்களை முடிக்க ஹோட்டல் லாபிகளில் அமர்ந்திருந்த நாட்கள் உண்டு. மற்ற நாட்களில், வீட்டில் இருந்தே வேலை செய்யும் வகையில், ஜெனரேட்டர்களுக்கு எரிபொருளைத் தேடி, கொழும்பில் ஓடினார்கள்.

"எரிபொருள் கிடைப்பது பற்றி ஒருவருக்கொருவர் தெரிவிக்க நிறுவனத்தில் ஒரு நண்பர் அமைப்பு உள்ளது," என்று சிரிக்கிறார் வைத்தியாலங்காரா, கொழும்பில் கூட்டங்களில் கலந்து கொள்ளவும், முடிந்தவரை வீட்டிலிருந்து வேலை செய்ய வேண்டியிருந்தால் கார்பூல் செய்ய ஊழியர்களை ஊக்குவிக்கிறது என்றும் கூறினார்.

தொற்றுநோய்க்கு முன்னர், இலங்கையின் தகவல் தொழில்நுட்பத் துறை 120,000 க்கும் அதிகமான மக்களைப் பணியமர்த்தியது மற்றும் 22 மில்லியன் தீவு தேசத்திற்கு ஐந்தாவது பெரிய ஏற்றுமதி வருமானம் ஈட்டியது. அடுத்த ஐந்து ஆண்டுகளில் சிறந்த ஏற்றுமதியாளராகி, அதன் ஊழியர்களை இரட்டிப்பாக்கும் பாதையில் அது இருந்தது. ஆனால் ஜனாதிபதி கோட்டாபய ராஜபக்ஷவின் அரசாங்கம் இந்த வருடத்தின் தொடக்கத்தில் தனது வெளிநாட்டுக் கடனைத் திருப்பிச் செலுத்தவில்லை மற்றும் கடுமையான பற்றாக்குறை பொருளாதாரத்தை முடக்கியதால், சாதாரண வணிக நடவடிக்கைகளைப் பராமரிப்பது கடினமாகிவிட்டதால், அந்த திட்டங்கள் இப்போது ஆபத்தில் உள்ளன.

தினசரி, மணிக்கணக்கான மின்வெட்டு தற்போது சாதாரணமாக உள்ளது. எரிபொருள் வரிசைகள் கிலோமீட்டர்கள் வரை நீண்டிருக்கும், சில சமயங்களில் ஒரு எரிபொருள் வரிசை மற்றொன்றைச் சந்திக்கும். பணப்புழக்கத்தின் அடிப்படையில் நாடு இயங்குகிறது. இலங்கை மத்திய வங்கியின் (CBSL) ஆளுனர் நந்தலால் வீரசிங்க, “எங்களால் இயன்ற அத்தியாவசியப் பொருட்களை கொள்வனவு செய்வதற்கு நாம் வரும் டாலர்களைப் பயன்படுத்துகிறோம்.

மே 19 அன்று, வாழ்க்கைச் செலவு தொடர்ந்து அதிகரித்து வருவதால் பொருளாதாரம் "ஒரு பின்னடைவை பதிவு செய்யும்" என்று எதிர்பார்ப்பதாக CBSL கூறியது - மே பணவீக்கம் 39.1 சதவீதமாக இருந்தது, மேலும் இந்த ஆண்டின் தொடக்கத்தில் இருந்து எரிபொருள் விலைகள் இருமடங்காக அதிகரித்துள்ளது.

தகவல் தொழில்நுட்பத் துறை திறம்பட செயல்படுவதற்கு அவசியமான நிலையான சக்தி மற்றும் சீரான இணையம் தொடர்ந்து பாதிக்கப்படுகிறது. "எங்களிடம் வாராந்திர மற்றும் மாதாந்திர டெலிவரிகள் உள்ளன, சில சமயங்களில் அவற்றைச் சந்திக்க நாங்கள் சிரமப்படுகிறோம்" என்று வைத்தியலங்காரா அல் ஜசீராவிடம் கூறினார். "எங்கள் சேவை நிலைகளும் பாதிக்கப்பட்டுள்ளன, எடுத்துக்காட்டாக, இணைய பாதுகாப்பு சம்பவத்திற்கு நாங்கள் இரண்டு மணி நேரத்திற்குள் பதிலளிக்க வேண்டும், ஆனால் மின்வெட்டு மற்றும் மெதுவான இணையத்துடன் இந்த எதிர்பார்ப்புகளை பூர்த்தி செய்வது சவாலாக உள்ளது," என்று அவர் கூறினார்.

இந்தியா, பங்களாதேஷ் மற்றும் வியட்நாம் உட்பட தகவல் தொழில்நுட்பத் துறையில் இலங்கைக்கு பல பிராந்திய போட்டியாளர்கள் உள்ளனர். "வாடிக்கையாளர்களுடன் உறவுகளை வளர்த்துக்கொள்வதில் நாங்கள் பல வருடங்களைச் செலவிட்டுள்ளோம், எனவே அவர்கள் புரிந்துகொள்கிறார்கள், ஆனால் எங்கள் விநியோகத்தையும் தரத்தையும் பராமரிக்க முடியாவிட்டால், எங்கள் வணிகம் பறிக்கப்பட்டு போட்டியாளர்களுக்கு வழங்கப்படும் என்ற அச்சம் எப்போதும் உள்ளது," என்கிறார் வைத்தியலங்காரா.
தற்போது நிலவும் பொருளாதார நெருக்கடியும், அதைத் தீர்ப்பதற்கு தற்போதைய அரசாங்கத்தின் ஒருங்கிணைந்த திட்டம் இல்லாததும் வெளிநாட்டு முதலீட்டாளர்களின் நம்பிக்கையைப் பாதிக்கத் தொடங்கியுள்ளது.

நவம்பரில் Goldman Sachs இலிருந்து $90m திரட்டிய இலங்கை மென்பொருள் நிறுவனமான WSO2 இன் நிறுவனர் மற்றும் பிரதம நிறைவேற்று அதிகாரி சஞ்சீவ வீரவரன அல் ஜசீராவிடம், முதலீட்டாளர்களை திருப்திப்படுத்துவதற்காக நாட்டின் அபாயத்தை தடுக்க ஆக்கப்பூர்வமான வழிகளை தனது நிறுவனம் தேட வேண்டியுள்ளது என்று கூறினார். அமெரிக்கா, ஐக்கிய இராச்சியம், துபாய் மற்றும் இந்தியா உட்பட பல நாடுகளில் WSO2 அலுவலகங்களைக் கொண்டிருக்கும் அதேவேளை, முதலீட்டாளர்களின் நம்பிக்கையை அதிகரிக்கவும் வர்த்தக தொடர்ச்சியை உறுதிப்படுத்தவும் நிறுவனம் இலங்கைக்கு வெளியே தனது பிரசன்னத்தை "விரிவாக்க" முடியும் என வீரவரண கூறினார்.

சில நிறுவனங்கள் இந்தியா மற்றும் துபாய் போன்ற அண்டை நாடுகளில் தற்காலிக அலுவலகங்களை அமைத்துள்ளன மற்றும் வணிக தொடர்ச்சியை பராமரிக்க தங்கள் ஊழியர்களில் சிலரை இந்த அலுவலகங்களுக்கு இடமாற்றம் செய்துள்ளன. "நாங்கள் நிறுவனத்தை இடமாற்றம் செய்யப் போவதில்லை, ஆனால் வணிகம் சீராக நடைபெறுவதை உறுதி செய்வதற்காக ஏப்ரல் மாதத்தில் சில ஊழியர்களை துபாய் அலுவலகத்திற்கு தற்காலிகமாக இடமாற்றம் செய்தோம்" என்று வீரவரண கூறினார்.

இலங்கையில் கடந்த சில வருடங்களாக தொல்லைகள் ஏற்பட்டுள்ளன. 2018 அக்டோபரில் 52 நாள் அரசமைப்புச் சட்டத்திற்கு முரணான அரசாங்கம் முதலீட்டாளர்களின் நம்பிக்கையை உலுக்கியது, சில மாதங்களுக்குப் பிறகு ஏப்ரல் 2019 இல் ஈஸ்டர் ஞாயிறு அன்று கொடிய குண்டுவெடிப்புச் சம்பவங்கள் நிகழ்ந்தன. மேலும், குறிப்பாக மென்பொருள் பொறியாளர்கள், மருத்துவர்கள் மற்றும் செவிலியர்கள் போன்ற மிகவும் திறமையான தொழிலாளர்களிடையே இடம்பெயர்வது அசாதாரணமானது அல்ல. கடந்த வருடத்தின் பொருளாதார நிலைமைகள், அதற்கு முன்னர் ஏற்பட்ட கொந்தளிப்பின் மேல், இலங்கையர்கள் பசுமையான மேய்ச்சல் நிலங்களைத் தேடும் வேகத்தை அதிகரித்துள்ளது.

குறிப்பாக கடந்த வருடத்தில் இலங்கை ரூபாவிற்கு எதிராக அமெரிக்க டொலரின் பெறுமதி 75 வீதத்தால் அதிகரித்துள்ள நிலையில் தற்போது நிலவும் பொருளாதார நெருக்கடி வாழ்க்கைச் செலவை மிகைப்படுத்தியுள்ளது. சொந்தமாக கார் அல்லது வீடொன்றை வைத்திருப்பது என்பது ஒரு கனவாகும், மேலும் அவர்களின் நம்பிக்கைகள் அவர்களைச் சுற்றி நொறுங்கிக்கொண்டிருக்கும் நிலையில், பல இலங்கை இளைஞர்கள் தங்கள் பிறந்த நாட்டில் நிச்சயமற்ற எதிர்காலத்திலிருந்து புலம்பெயர்ந்து வெளியேற விரும்புகிறார்கள்.

கொழும்பை தளமாகக் கொண்ட இன்ஸ்டிட்யூட் ஃபார் ஹெல்த் பாலிசியின் நவம்பர் 2021 கணக்கெடுப்பு அறிக்கை (PDF), இடம்பெயர விரும்பும் இலங்கையர்களின் எண்ணிக்கை மூன்றிலிருந்து ஐந்து ஆண்டுகளுக்கு முன்பு இரட்டிப்பாகியுள்ளது மற்றும் கிட்டத்தட்ட 50 சதவீத இளைஞர்கள் மற்றும் படித்தவர்கள் இப்போது இடம்பெயர விரும்பினர். பாஸ்போர்ட் அலுவலகத்தில் நீண்ட வரிசைகள் இதற்கு ஒரு அறிகுறியாகும்.

பெயர் குறிப்பிட விரும்பாத 35 வயதான மென்பொருள் பொறியாளர், இந்த ஆண்டு மார்ச் மாதம் தனது குடும்பத்துடன் ஆஸ்திரேலியா சென்றார். “எனக்கு இடம்பெயரும் திட்டம் எதுவும் இல்லை. நான் ஒரு சிறிய ஐடி நிறுவனத்தை நடத்தினேன், அதில் நான் மகிழ்ச்சியடைந்தேன், ”என்று அவர் கூறினார். ஆனால் 2019 தேர்தலுக்கு முன்னர் நாட்டில் இன மற்றும் இனப் பதட்டங்கள் அதிகரித்தது, அவரை இடமாற்றம் செய்வது பற்றி சிந்திக்க வைத்தது மற்றும் அதன் பின்னர் ஏற்பட்ட பொருளாதார தவறான நிர்வாகம் அவரது முடிவுக்கு முத்திரை குத்தியது. “இது உண்மையில் இலங்கையில் நம்பிக்கையளிக்கவில்லை. எனது மகளின் எதிர்காலத்திற்கு முன்னுரிமை அளிக்க விரும்புகிறேன்,'' என்றார்.

லண்டன் ஸ்டாக் எக்ஸ்சேஞ்ச் மற்றும் போர்சா இத்தாலினா போன்ற உலகின் மிகப் பெரிய பங்குச் சந்தைகளில் சிலவற்றையும், குவாண்டாஸ் போன்ற உலகின் பரபரப்பான சில விமான நிறுவனங்களையும் இலங்கை சாப்ட்வேர் நிறுவனங்களால் இயக்க முடியும் என்றால், அதற்கு உள்ளூர் திறமைதான் காரணம். ஒரு மூளை வடிகால் துறைக்கு பேரழிவை ஏற்படுத்தும்.

"உலகத் தரம் வாய்ந்த நிறுவனங்களை உருவாக்க எங்களுக்கு உதவியவர்கள் எங்கள் மக்கள்" என்கிறார் வைத்தியலங்காரா. "எங்கள் தொழில்துறைக்கான உண்மையான போட்டி விளிம்பு மக்களிடமிருந்து வருகிறது, துரதிர்ஷ்டவசமாக அவர்கள் இப்போது நாட்டை விட்டு வெளியேறத் தொடங்கியுள்ளனர். இது ஒரு பெரிய இழப்பு."

வீரவரன ஒப்புக்கொண்டார். "எங்கள் தொழில்நுட்ப ரீதியாக திறமையான மற்றும் அனுபவம் வாய்ந்த ஊழியர்களில் 10 சதவீதம் பேர் ஏற்கனவே இடம்பெயர்வுக்கு விண்ணப்பித்துள்ளனர், மேலும் 20-40 சதவீதம் பேர் அதை பரிசீலித்து வருகின்றனர்" என்று அவர் கூறினார்.

இத்தொழில் மற்ற வழிகளிலும் முக்கியமானது - இந்த ஆண்டு மார்ச் மற்றும் ஏப்ரல் மாதங்களில் ராஜபக்சே அரசுக்கு எதிராக இலங்கை முழுவதும் தன்னெழுச்சியான போராட்ட அலைகளை பகிரங்கமாக ஆதரித்த முதல் நிறுவனங்களில் இதுவும் ஒன்றாகும். தலைநகரின் காலி முகத்திடல் நடைபாதையில் உள்ளூர் மக்கள் 'கோட்டகோகம' தளத்தை அமைத்ததில் இருந்து 50 நாட்கள் போராட்டத்தை குறிக்கும் வகையில் தொழில்துறை ஊழியர்கள் ஒரு பேரணியை ஏற்பாடு செய்தனர்.

அதிக சம்பளம் மற்றும் நெகிழ்வான பணிச்சூழலுடன், இலங்கையின் தகவல் தொழில்நுட்பத் துறையானது வங்கி மற்றும் சுற்றுலா போன்ற முக்கியமான துறைகளை விட சிறந்த திறமைகளை எப்போதும் கொண்டுள்ளது. இப்போது அந்த திறமையை தக்கவைக்க, தொழில்துறை வீரர்கள் புதிய வழிகளை வகுக்க வேண்டியிருந்தது.

இலங்கை ரூபாய் வீழ்ச்சியடையத் தொடங்கியதில் இருந்து, வெளிநாட்டு நாணயங்களில் சம்பாதிக்கும் பல தகவல் தொழில்நுட்ப நிறுவனங்கள் தங்கள் உள்ளூர் ஊழியர்களின் சம்பளத்தை அமெரிக்க டாலர், பவுண்ட் ஸ்டெர்லிங், யூரோ அல்லது ஆஸ்திரேலிய டாலர் ஆகியவற்றுடன் இணைக்கத் தொடங்கியுள்ளன - இந்தத் தொழிலை பலருக்கு கவர்ச்சிகரமான முதலாளியாக மாற்றுகிறது. உள்ளூர் மக்கள்.

இது உள்ளூர் வாடிக்கையாளர்களை பூர்த்தி செய்யும் சிறிய தகவல் தொழில்நுட்ப நிறுவனங்களில் எதிர்பாராத விளைவை ஏற்படுத்தியது மற்றும் வெளிநாட்டு ஒப்பந்தங்கள் மற்றும் நாணயங்களின் பெரிய குழாய் இல்லை. பெரிய நிறுவனங்களைப் போலல்லாமல், அவர்கள் தங்கள் ஊழியர்களின் சம்பளத்தை வெளிநாட்டு நாணயத்துடன் இணைக்க முடியாது.

"சில முக்கிய பணியாளர்கள் இடம்பெயர்வு பற்றி பரிசீலித்து வருகின்றனர் அல்லது டாலர் மதிப்பிலான சம்பளத்தை வழங்கும் பெரிய நிறுவனங்களுக்குச் செல்கின்றனர்" என்று ஒரு சிறிய மென்பொருள் நிறுவனத்தின் உரிமையாளரான டீன் ஜெயமான்னே அல் ஜசீராவிடம் கூறினார். "மின்வெட்டு மற்றும் இணைய பிரச்சனைகளுக்கு மேல், இதையும் நாம் இப்போது சமாளிக்க வேண்டும்," என்று அவர் மேலும் கூறினார்.

அவுஸ்திரேலியாவிற்கு புலம்பெயர்ந்த மென்பொருள் பொறியியலாளர், இலங்கையில் உள்ள தகவல் தொழில்நுட்பத் துறையில் திறமைகளைப் பெறுதல் மற்றும் தக்கவைத்தல் கொள்கைகள் சரியானதாக இல்லை என்று தான் நினைக்கவில்லை என்று கூறினார். “[ஒரு வருடத்திற்கு முன்பு] நான் இடம்பெயர்வதற்காக வேலைகளுக்கு விண்ணப்பித்தபோது, ​​ஆஸ்திரேலியா மற்றும் நெதர்லாந்தில் இருந்து எனக்கு இரண்டு சலுகைகள் கிடைத்தன. அவர்கள் எல்லாவற்றையும் கவனித்துக்கொண்டார்கள். விசா, விமான டிக்கெட்டுகள், இடமாற்ற செலவுகள் மற்றும் தற்காலிக வீடுகள் கூட. நான் செய்ய வேண்டியதெல்லாம் விமான நிலையத்தில் காட்ட வேண்டும். திறமையைப் பெறுவதற்கு அவர்கள் எவ்வளவு தூரம் செல்கிறார்கள்.

நாட்டில் முதலீட்டை ஊக்குவிப்பதற்காக இலங்கையின் உச்ச அமைப்பான முதலீட்டுச் சபை கடந்த ஆண்டு IT துறையை நாட்டிற்கு அதிக வருமானம் அளிப்பவராகவும், வேலை வழங்குபவராகவும் மாற்றுவதற்கு லட்சியத் திட்டங்களை வகுத்த போதிலும், உண்மை வேறு வழியில் செல்வதாகத் தெரிகிறது.

தொழில்துறையில் ஏற்கனவே விநியோக இடைவெளி இருந்ததால் நிலைமை மோசமாக உள்ளது. நாட்டில் ICT கொள்கைக்கு பொறுப்பான அரசாங்க அமைப்பான தகவல் மற்றும் தொடர்பாடல் தொழில்நுட்ப நிறுவனம், 2019 ஆம் ஆண்டு அறிக்கையில் "ஐசிடி ஊழியர்களுக்கான தேவை-விநியோக இடைவெளி மூடுவதற்குப் பதிலாக விரிவடைகிறது" என்று எச்சரித்தது.

டாலரால் நிர்ணயிக்கப்பட்ட சம்பளம் மற்றும் நெகிழ்வான பணிச்சூழல் இருந்தாலும், தற்போதைய பொருளாதார நெருக்கடிக்கு விரைவான தீர்வு என்பது திறமையைத் தக்கவைக்கத் தேவை என்று முதலாளிகள் கூறுகின்றனர். வெளிநாட்டு முதலீட்டாளர்கள் மற்றும் வாடிக்கையாளர்களின் நிலையான குழாய்வழியை உறுதிசெய்வதற்கும் இது முக்கியமானது. "நாட்டிற்கான பாடத்திட்டத்தை நாங்கள் பட்டியலிட்டவுடன், முதலீட்டாளர்களின் பிரச்சனைகள் கணிசமாக குறையும் என்று நான் நம்புகிறேன்," என்று வீரவரன கூறினார்.

அந்த தீர்வு இல்லாமல், நாடு "அதன் எதிர்கால வளர்ச்சியை குறைமதிப்பிற்கு உட்படுத்தும்" என்று நிபுணர்கள் கவலைப்படுகிறார்கள். பொருளாதார வல்லுனர் மாலதி நைட், அல் ஜசீராவிற்கு அளித்த பேட்டியில், லெபனானை சுட்டிக்காட்டினார், அதன் சொந்த பொருளாதார மற்றும் அரசியல் நெருக்கடிக்கு முகங்கொடுக்கும் வகையில் உள்ளூர் திறமைகள் குறிப்பிடத்தக்க அளவில் வெளியேறுவதைக் கண்டது. "இலங்கை மற்ற நாடுகளின் அனுபவங்களிலிருந்து கற்றுக்கொள்ள வேண்டும்," என்று அவர் கூறினார்.

"இளைஞர்களுக்கு நம்பிக்கை மற்றும் வாய்ப்புகளை வழங்குவது இன்றியமையாதது" என்று நைட் கூறினார், "அத்தகைய முயற்சிகள் இல்லாமல், பொருளாதார நெருக்கடி நிலைபெற்ற பின்னரும் இலங்கை பொருளாதார ரீதியாகவும் சமூக ரீதியாகவும் தொடர்ந்து கீழ்நோக்கிச் செல்லும்."

en.title: Exodus  of Sri Lanka’s Talented IT Professionals May Gather Momentum if the Island Nation’s Economic Crisis Shows No Sign Of Being Resolved
body_lines: There have been days when cybersecurity professional Asela Waidyalankara and his colleagues have sat in hotel lobbies to complete projects during power outages. Other days, they have run around Colombo looking for fuel for generators so they could work from home.

“We have a buddy system at the company to inform each other about fuel availability,” laughs Waidyalankara, adding that his company encourages staff to carpool if they have to attend meetings in Colombo and work from home when possible.

Before the pandemic, Sri Lanka’s IT industry employed more than 120,000 people and was the fifth-largest export earner for the island nation of 22 million. It was on track to become the top exporter within the next five years and double its employees. But with the government of President Gotabaya Rajapaksa having defaulted on its foreign debt earlier this year and critical shortages crippling the economy, those plans are now in jeopardy as it becomes harder to maintain normal business operations.

Daily, hours-long power cuts are now normal. Fuel queues stretch for kilometres, sometimes so far that one fuel queue meets another. The country is running on a cash flow basis. “We are using whatever dollars that flow in to purchase the essentials we can,” Nandalal Weerasinghe, governor of the Central Bank of Sri Lanka (CBSL), said last month.

On May 19, the CBSL said it expects the economy to “record a setback” even as the cost of living continues to shoot up – May inflation was 39.1 percent, and fuel prices have more than doubled since the start of the year.

Steady power and smooth internet, essential for the IT industry to function effectively, are constantly affected. “We have weekly and monthly deliverables and we struggle to meet them sometimes,” Waidyalankara told Al Jazeera. “Our service levels are also affected, for example, we have to reply to a cybersecurity incident within two hours, but with power cuts and slow internet it is a challenge to meet these expectations,” he said.

Sri Lanka has several regional competitors in the IT sector, including India, Bangladesh and Vietnam. “We have spent years cultivating relationships with clients, so they are understanding, but there is always a fear that our business will be taken away and given to competitors if we cannot maintain our delivery and quality,” says Waidyalankara.
The ongoing economic crisis and the lack of a cohesive plan by the incumbent government to resolve it are starting to affect the confidence of foreign investors.

Sanjiva Weerawarana, founder and chief executive of WSO2, a Sri Lankan software company which raised $90m from Goldman Sachs in November, told Al Jazeera that his company has had to look for creative ways to hedge the country’s risk to appease investors. While WSO2 has offices in several countries, including the United States, the United Kingdom, Dubai and India, the company will “likely expand” its presence outside Sri Lanka to boost investor confidence and ensure business continuity, Weerawarana said.

Some companies have even set up temporary offices in neighbouring countries like India and Dubai and relocated some of their staff to these offices to maintain business continuity. “We are not going to relocate the company, but we temporarily relocated a few staff members to a Dubai office in April just to ensure business runs smoothly,” said Weerawarana.

The past few years in Sri Lanka have been troublesome. An unconstitutional 52-day government in October 2018 shook investor confidence and was followed a few months later by deadly bomb blasts on Easter Sunday in April 2019. And while migration, especially among highly skilled workers like software engineers, doctors and nurses is not uncommon, the economic conditions of the past year, on top of the turmoil before that, have increased the pace at which Sri Lankans seek greener pastures.

The ongoing economic crisis has made the cost of living exorbitant, especially as the value of the US dollar has appreciated by 75 percent against the Sri Lankan rupee in the past year. Owning a car or a house is a pipe dream and with their hopes crumbling around them, many young Sri Lankans are looking to migrate and get away from an uncertain future in their birth country.

A November 2021 survey report (PDF) by the Colombo-based Institute for Health Policy, an independent research centre, found that the number of Sri Lankans who want to migrate had doubled from three to five years ago and nearly 50 percent of the young and educated wanted to migrate now. Long queues at the passport office are just one indication of this.

A 35-year old software engineer who preferred not to be named moved to Australia with his family in March this year. “I didn’t really have any plans to migrate. I ran a small IT company, and I was happy with it,” he said. But a rise in ethnic and racial tensions in the country before the 2019 elections made him think about relocating and the subsequent economic mismanagement sealed his decision. “It’s really not promising in Sri Lanka. I want to prioritize my daughter’s future,” he said.

If Sri Lankan software companies can power some of the world’s largest stock markets like the London Stock Exchange and Borsa Italiana, and some of the world’s busiest airlines like Qantas, it is because of local talent. A brain drain would spell disaster for the sector.

“It’s our people who have helped us build world-class companies,” says Waidyalankara. “The real competitive edge for our industry comes from people, and unfortunately they are now beginning to move out of the country. It is a big loss.”
Weerawarana agrees. “We believe that 10 percent of our technically skilled and experienced staff have already applied for migration and another 20-40 percent are probably considering it,” he said.

The industry has also been important in other ways – it was one of the first to publicly support the wave of spontaneous protests held across Sri Lanka in March and April this year against the Rajapaksa government. Industry personnel even organized a rally to mark 50 days of protests since locals set up the ‘GotaGoGama’ site at the capital’s Galle Face Green promenade.

With its high salaries and flexible working environment, Sri Lanka’s IT industry has always had a better draw of talent than other important sectors like banking and tourism. To retain that talent now, industry players have had to devise newer ways.

Since the Sri Lankan rupee began plummeting, several IT companies which earn in foreign currencies have begun pegging the salaries of their local staff to either the US dollar, the pound sterling, the euro or the Australian dollar – making the industry an attractive employer for many locals.

This has had an unexpected effect on the smaller IT firms which cater to local clients and lack a large pipeline of foreign contracts and currencies. Unlike the larger companies, they are not able to peg the salaries of their staff to a foreign currency.

“Some of the key staff are considering migration or are moving into the larger companies who pay dollar-pegged salaries,” Deane Jayamanne, an owner of a small software company, told Al Jazeera. “On top of power cuts and internet problems, we also have to deal with this now,” he added.

The software engineer who migrated to Australia said that he did not think the IT industry in Sri Lanka had proper talent acquisition and retention policies. “When I applied for jobs to migrate [a year ago], I got two offers from Australia and the Netherlands. They took care of everything. The visa, air tickets, relocation expenses and even temporary housing. All I had to do was show up at the airport. That is how far they go to acquire talent.”

Despite ambitious plans last year by Sri Lanka’s apex body for investment promotion in the country, the Board of Investment, to push the IT industry as a top earner and employer for the nation, the reality seems to be heading another way.

The situation is aggravated because there was already an existing supply gap in the industry. The Information and Communication Technology Agency, the government body responsible for ICT policy in the country, warned in a 2019 report that “the demand-supply gap for ICT workers is widening rather than closing”.

Even with dollar-pegged salaries and flexible work environments, a quick resolution to the ongoing economic crisis is what is needed to retain talent, employers say. It is also the key to ensuring a steady pipeline of foreign investors and clients. “Once we have charted a course for the country, I believe investor trouble will go down significantly,” said Weerawarana.

Experts worry that without that solution, the country “risks undermining its future growth”. Economist Malathy Knight, in an interview with Al Jazeera, pointed to Lebanon, which saw a significant exodus of local talent in the face of its own economic and political crisis. “Sri Lanka should learn from the experiences of other countries,” she said.

“It is vital to provide hope and opportunities to the youth”, Knight said, “without such efforts, Sri Lanka will continue to spiral downward economically and socially even after the economic crisis stabilizes.”
